openmeter/entitlement/validators/custome.purpose: Implements customer.RequestValidator to block deletion of customers that still have active entitlements. Single-file bridge between the entitlement and customer domains that avoids circular imports by depending on entitlement.EntitlementRepo instead of entitlement.Service.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Always declare var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil) at package level so interface drift is caught at compile time.

patterns.name: Embed NoopRequestValidator for default methods
patterns.description: Embed customer.NoopRequestValidator in the Validator struct to get safe no-op defaults for any RequestValidator methods not explicitly overridden, preventing breakage when the interface adds methods.
patterns.example: type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; entitlementRepo entitlement.EntitlementRepo }

patterns.name: Constructor nil-guard returning error
patterns.description: NewValidator returns (*Validator, error) and rejects nil dependencies with a descriptive error before constructing the struct.
patterns.example: if entitlementRepo == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("entitlement repository is required") }

patterns.name: GenericConflictError for blocked deletions
patterns.description: Return models.NewGenericConflictError when a customer cannot be deleted due to active dependents; the HTTP GenericErrorEncoder maps this to 409.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("customer %s still has active entitlements", input.ID))

patterns.name: clock.Now() for testable time
patterns.description: Use clock.Now() (not time.Now()) when computing the current time for ActiveAt filtering so tests can inject a fake clock.
patterns.example: now := clock.Now(); ActiveAt: lo.ToPtr(now), IncludeDeletedAfter: now

patterns.name: Depend on EntitlementRepo, not entitlement.Service
patterns.description: Use entitlement.EntitlementRepo (low-level repo interface) to avoid a heavy dependency on the full service graph and prevent import cycles with the customer domain.

key_file_guides.role: Only file in the package. Defines Validator, its constructor, and the ValidateDeleteCustomer pre-mutation guard. Future RequestValidator methods (ValidateCreateCustomer, ValidateUpdateCustomer) would be added here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not call entitlement.Service — only entitlement.EntitlementRepo. Always embed NoopRequestValidator. Return models.GenericConflictError (not GenericValidationError) for 'has active dependents'.

anti_patterns: Importing entitlement.Service instead of entitlement.EntitlementRepo — creates import cycles and a heavier dependency graph.
Returning a non-GenericConflictError for 'has active entitlements' — the encoder maps only GenericConflictError to 409.
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() — makes tests non-deterministic.
Adding state-transition or post-mutation logic here — this package is exclusively for pre-mutation blocking guards.
Omitting the compile-time interface assertion — silent breakage if customer.RequestValidator gains methods.

decisions.decision: Depend on entitlement.EntitlementRepo rather than entitlement.Service.
decisions.rationale: entitlement.Service transitively imports customer, creating a circular dependency; the repo interface satisfies the validation need without pulling in the full service graph.

decisions.decision: Embed customer.NoopRequestValidator instead of implementing all methods.
decisions.rationale: Provides safe no-op defaults so adding a method to the interface does not silently break this validator until it needs a real implementation.

code_examples.scenario: Add ValidateUpdateCustomer that blocks updates when active entitlements exist
